title: RnS v2.1: declared-idle protocol and 2h check-in nudges
description: Implement IDEA-0007/PROMO-0006 per operator #15495: (1) scripts/declare_standby.py -- agents declare standby/awaiting_work when their queue is empty, SQLite-first then export (mirror lesson); (2) watcher check-in path -- live agents with no IN_PROGRESS claim, durable status available, and hcom idle 2h+ get a message-only nudge (never a spawn), at most once per 2h window; (3) safety boundaries from the idea card enforced: declared standby suppresses, claims suppress, no auto work assignment, no headless anything.
acceptance_criteria: decide_checkins is pure and tested: claim suppresses, declaration suppresses, short idle suppresses, 2h+ undeclared idle nudges, renudge throttled to one per window
declare_standby.py writes SQLite first and exports; --back returns agent to available
Check-ins are hcom messages only -- no hcom r, no session spawns
Dry-run against live state before restart; suite passes
objective: Give MAP the declared-idle primitive: agents say when they're intentionally done, and the watcher politely checks in on live agents that drift 2+ hours with no claim and no declaration.
required_context: SYN-0001 mirror lesson: SQLite is the source; status.json is an export
forbidden_changes: No session spawns from the check-in path (message only)
No automatic work assignment in nudge text or behavior
No nudges to agents with a declared reason, an IN_PROGRESS claim, or non-available durable status
No direct status.json writes (SQLite first, then export)
expected_artifacts: live dry-run evidence (zero false nudges on current state) before restart
risk: Low-medium: worst case is noisy messages, not spawned sessions. Mitigations: 2h threshold, once-per-window throttle, dry-run-first live validation per limo's experiment framing.
